resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def hasAlternatingBits(self, n: int) -> bool:
        prev_bit = n & 1
        n >>= 1
        while n:
            current_bit = n & 1
            if current_bit == prev_bit:
                return False
            prev_bit = current_bit
            n >>= 1
        return True
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates through the bits of the input integer from right to left. It compares each bit with the previous bit, returning False if they are the same. Otherwise, it updates the previous bit and moves to the next bit. This approach has a time complexity of O(log n) because it processes each bit in the integer, and the number of bits is logarithmic to the integer value. It uses constant space, making the space complexity O(1).
